title: mertensia lanceolata var. fendleri
intro: Mertensia lanceolata var. fendleri, also known as Fendler's bluebells, is a perennial flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae) and is characterized by its blue, bell-shaped flowers and lance-shaped leaves. Mertensia lanceolata var. fendleri has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce pain and swelling, as well as promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant include tea and tincture, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin irritations.
